label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to answer the question correctly. The question is asking which of the four options is TRUE based on the information given in the passage. Let's analyze each option one by one: **(A) Computers can solve all the problems people can't do.** The passage says, "Computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines." But it does not say that computers can solve all the problems that people can't do. That's an overstatement. So, (A) is not TRUE. **(B) Large computers can only solve one problem at a time.** The passage mentions, "Scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time." This means that large computers can solve multiple problems simultaneously, which contradicts option (B) that says "only one problem at a time." So, (B) is not TRUE. **(C) When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything.** The passage explains the process of using the Kurzweil machine: "a blind person can simply open the book and put it down on the scanner. The machine 'reads' the words on the page. When one page is finished, the blind person turns the page and puts the book on the scanner again." So, the blind person needs to turn the page and place the book on the scanner again. Therefore, the blind person does need to do something. This makes option (C)
